name: William Booth
title: Evangelist & Social Reformer
bio: English Methodist preacher who co-founded The Salvation Army with his wife Catherine in 1865. The organization combined evangelism with social services for the poor and has grown into a worldwide movement operating in over 130 countries.
faith: Booth's passion was bringing the gospel to the poorest and most neglected members of society. He famously said, 'While women weep, as they do now, I'll fight; while little children go hungry, as they do now, I'll fight.' The Salvation Army's model of practical Christianity has served millions.

sources.title: William Booth — Wikipedia
sources.note: Biography of the founder of The Salvation Army

sources.title: In Darkest England and the Way Out — William Booth
sources.note: Booth's landmark manifesto calling for Christian social action — it launched the Salvation Army's comprehensive approach to poverty, addiction, and homelessness

sources.title: The Salvation Army Heritage Centre
sources.note: Official archives of the Salvation Army documenting Booth's founding vision and the movement's growth from London street preaching to a global Christian mission

tagline: Founded the Salvation Army to fight sin and save souls
